page: Microsoft Azure E16ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Epsv6 server family. Powered by a Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz, this instance features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs with no hyperthreading. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The processor includes 128 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache per core, and 64 KB of L1 instruction cache. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it is Premium Storage capable.

In benchmark testing, the E16ps_v6 demonstrates strong memory performance. Its Passmark memory score ranks in the top tier, supported by uncached large-block memory read bandwidth of 308.13 GB/sec and write bandwidth of 124.58 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. Single-threaded gzip compression is also strong at 33.21 GB/sec. Other compute metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and multi-core performance, fall within the average range. The server achieves an average Redis SET performance of 32.32 million operations per second and static web serving throughput of 28.78 GB/sec.

The ARM64 architecture of the Neoverse-N2 processor provides high resource density and memory capacity, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads compared to traditional x86 alternatives. However, the lack of bundled local storage requires external network-attached storage, and the absence of AVX or AMX instruction sets limits its suitability for heavy vector math or large-scale AI workloads, as evidenced by its weak prompt processing speed of 24.62 tokens/sec on 7B LLM models. This server is best suited for memory-optimized workloads, including relational databases, caching layers like Redis, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E16ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Epsv6 family, featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio, but contains no bundled local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmark results highlight top-tier memory performance, with a Passmark memory score in the top 10% and uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 308.13 GB/sec. While single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks sit in the average tier, the instance excels in memory-bound operations, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for memory-intensive workloads. It is highly suited for database hosting, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring high memory density without the need for local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores and 128 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth for databases and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16ps_v6 is an ARM-based VM with 16 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine delivering high memory density and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARM64 Neoverse-N2 cores running at 3.4 GHz
128.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier memory performance with uncached read bandwidth of 308.13 GB/sec
No bundled local storage, requiring external Premium Storage
Optimized for relational databases, Redis caching, and memory-heavy applications
